description: Stock analysis dashboard — technical indicators, fundamental scores, and market data from yfinance → InfluxDB

panels.description: Chat-style financial Q&A. Ask natural-language questions about stock data in InfluxDB — the AI generates Flux queries, executes them, and formats the answer.

options.prompt:
You are an expert data analyst reviewing Grafana panel data.

Given the panel configuration and query results below, provide:
1. A summary of what metrics/data are being displayed
2. Key trends or patterns in the data
3. Any anomalies or notable observations
4. Actionable insights or recommendations

Be concise but thorough. Use bullet points for clarity.

panels.title: Ask AI

panels.description: Business summary, industry classification, and last-updated timestamp for each tracked stock.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "company_info")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => exists r["industry"] and r["industry"] != "")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> group()
  |> unique(column: "ticker")
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "ticker", "industry"])

panels.title: Company Details

panels.description: Latest trade price from [yfinance](https://pypi.org/project/yfinance/). Updated each export cycle.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "current_price")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Current Price

panels.description: [Price-to-Earnings ratio](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/p/price-earningsratio.asp) (trailing 12 months). Lower values may indicate undervaluation relative to earnings; high values suggest growth expectations or overvaluation. Negative = unprofitable.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "pe_ratio")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: P/E Ratio

panels.description: Highest trade price in the past 52 weeks. Proximity may indicate [resistance](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/resistance.asp) or a breakout.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "week_52_high")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: 52-Week High

panels.description: Lowest trade price in the past 52 weeks. Proximity may indicate [support](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/s/support.asp) or further downside.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "week_52_low")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: 52-Week Low

panels.description: [Market capitalization](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/m/marketcapitalization.asp) = share price × shares outstanding. Micro (<$300M), Small (<$2B), Mid (<$10B), Large (<$200B), Mega (>$200B).

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "market_cap")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Market Cap

panels.description: [Piotroski F-Score](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/p/piotroski-score.asp) (0–9). Measures financial strength across profitability, leverage, liquidity, and operating efficiency. ≥7 = strong fundamentals; ≤3 = weakness.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "piotroski_score")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Piotroski Score

panels.description: [Implied volatility](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/i/iv.asp) (IV) from nearest-expiry ATM call option. Higher IV = market expects larger price swings. Useful for [options](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/o/option.asp) pricing and gauging uncertainty.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "iv")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Implied Volatility

panels.description: [Bollinger Bands](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/b/bollingerbands.asp) signal (Buy / Hold / Sell). Buy = price near lower band (potential [support](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/s/support.asp)); Sell = price near upper band (potential [resistance](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/resistance.asp)).

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "current_price")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_value: r.bollinger_signal, ticker: r.ticker}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Bollinger Signal

panels.description: [Bid-ask spread](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/b/bid-askspread.asp) as a percentage of the midpoint price. Lower = more liquid; wider spreads indicate lower liquidity or higher volatility. Useful for assessing [market depth](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/m/marketdepth.asp) and trading costs.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "bid_ask_spread_pct")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Bid/Ask Spread %

panels.description: [Implied volatility](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/i/iv.asp) alongside current price for all tracked tickers. Helps identify stocks where options markets expect large moves relative to their price level.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "current_price" or r["_field"] == "iv")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> pivot(rowKey: ["_time", "ticker"], columnKey: ["_field"], valueColumn: "_value")
  |> group()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "current_price", "iv"])
  |> sort(columns: ["iv"], desc: true)

panels.title: IV vs Price

panels.description: Calendar days until the next [earnings date](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/e/earningsreport.asp) from yfinance. Stocks with imminent reports appear first.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "days_until_earnings")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Days Until Earnings

panels.description: Consensus [EPS](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/e/eps.asp) estimate for the upcoming quarter from analyst forecasts via yfinance. Positive = profitable expectations; negative = expected loss.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "earnings_estimate_avg")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: EPS Estimate (Avg)

panels.description: Consensus [revenue](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/revenue.asp) estimate for the upcoming quarter from analyst forecasts via yfinance.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "revenue_estimate_avg")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))

panels.title: Revenue Estimate

panels.description: Most recent earnings-related price reaction per ticker. Shows the percentage change from the day before to the day after the latest earnings report.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: -2y)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "earnings_price_change")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "pct_change")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> group()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_time: r._time, _value: r._value, ticker: r.ticker}))
  |> sort(columns: ["_value"], desc: true)

panels.title: Earnings-Related Price Changes (Latest)

panels.description: Daily close overlaid with [Simple Moving Averages](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/s/sma.asp) (50, 100, 150, 200-day). Price crossing above/below an MA may signal trend changes. The 200-day MA is a widely-watched long-term trend indicator.

targets.query:
import "strings"

from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "close" or r["_field"] == "ma_50" or r["_field"] == "ma_100" or r["_field"] == "ma_150" or r["_field"] == "ma_200")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field: if r["_field"] == "close" then r["ticker"] + " at Close" else r["ticker"] + " MA" + strings.substring(v: r["_field"], start: 3, end: strings.strlen(v: r["_field"]))}))
  |> drop(columns: ["ticker"])

panels.title: Price & Moving Averages

panels.description: Uses the Price History Range dropdown instead of the dashboard time picker

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: -${price_range})
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "close")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field: r.ticker}))
  |> drop(columns: ["ticker"])

panels.title: Price History — All Tickers (${price_range})

panels.description: [Relative Strength Index](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/rsi.asp) (14-period). Oscillator 0–100. >70 = [overbought](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/o/overbought.asp) (potential pullback); <30 = [oversold](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/o/oversold.asp) (potential bounce). Shaded zones highlight these regions.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "rsi")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field: r["ticker"] + " RSI"}))
  |> drop(columns: ["ticker"])

panels.title: RSI (14)

panels.description: [Moving Average Convergence Divergence](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/m/macd.asp). MACD crossing above the signal line = bullish; below = bearish. Histogram bars show the gap — growing bars = strengthening momentum.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "macd" or r["_field"] == "macd_signal")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field: if r["_field"] == "macd_signal" then r["ticker"] + " MACD SIGNAL" else r["ticker"] + " MACD"}))
  |> drop(columns: ["ticker"])

from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "macd_histogram")

panels.title: MACD

panels.description: [Volume Rate of Change](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/v/volumepricetrend.asp) — % change in volume over a lookback period. Spikes may confirm price moves (high conviction) or signal reversals.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "vroc")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field: if r["_field"] == "vroc" then r["ticker"] + " VROC" else r["ticker"] + " VROC"}))
  |> drop(columns: ["ticker"])

panels.title: VROC

targets.query:
import "join"

rsi = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "rsi")
  |> group()
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "ticker"])

industries = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "company_info")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => exists r["industry"] and r["industry"] != "")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> group()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "industry"])

join.inner(
  left: rsi,
  right: industries,
  on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker,
  as: (l, r) => ({_time: l._time, _value: l._value, industry: r.industry})
)
  |> group(columns: ["industry"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1d, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

import "join"

iv = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "iv")
  |> group()
  |> keep(columns: ["_time", "_value", "ticker"])

industries = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "company_info")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => exists r["industry"] and r["industry"] != "")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> group()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "industry"])

join.inner(
  left: iv,
  right: industries,
  on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker,
  as: (l, r) => ({_time: l._time, _value: l._value, industry: r.industry})
)
  |> group(columns: ["industry"])
  |> aggregateWindow(every: 1d, fn: mean, createEmpty: false)

panels.description: Distribution of analyst ratings (Strong Buy → Strong Sell) from [EODHD](https://eodhd.com/). Stacked bars show the sentiment balance per ticker.

targets.query:
strong_buy = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "analyst_strong_buy")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_value: r._value, ticker: r.ticker, _field: "Strong Buy"}))
  |> group()

buy = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "analyst_buy")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_value: r._value, ticker: r.ticker, _field: "Buy"}))
  |> group()

hold = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "analyst_hold")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_value: r._value, ticker: r.ticker, _field: "Hold"}))
  |> group()

sell = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "analyst_sell")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_value: r._value, ticker: r.ticker, _field: "Sell"}))
  |> group()

strong_sell = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "analyst_strong_sell")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({_value: r._value, ticker: r.ticker, _field: "Strong Sell"}))
  |> group()

union(tables: [strong_buy, buy, hold, sell, strong_sell])

panels.title: Analyst Consensus

panels.description: [EPS](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/e/eps.asp) actual vs analyst consensus estimate. Points above the estimate line = positive surprise (beat); below = miss.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_earnings")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "epsActual" or r["_field"] == "epsEstimate")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field: if r["_field"] == "epsActual" then r["ticker"] + " Actual" else r["ticker"] + " Estimate"}))
  |> drop(columns: ["ticker"])

panels.title: Earnings Surprise — EPS Actual vs Estimate

panels.description: [Short ratio](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/s/shortinterestratio.asp) (days to cover) = short interest ÷ avg daily volume. Higher = more days for shorts to unwind, increasing [short squeeze](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/s/shortsqueeze.asp) potential.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "short_ratio")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Short Ratio

panels.description: % of [float](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/f/float.asp) sold short. >10% = elevated; >20% = very high — bearish sentiment or squeeze risk.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")

  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])

  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))

panels.title: Short % of Float

panels.description: [Net profit margin](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/n/net_margin.asp) = net income ÷ revenue. Higher is better; negative = losing money.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "profit_margin")

panels.title: Profit Margin

panels.description: [ROE](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/returnonequity.asp) = net income ÷ shareholders' equity. Measures how efficiently profits are generated from equity. Above 15–20% is generally strong.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "return_on_equity")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Return on Equity (ROE)

panels.description:
Entry-point screener using the RSI + MACD crossover methodology.

**Buy Signal** fires when:
1. RSI is oversold (< 30)
2. MACD has crossed above its signal line (bullish crossover)

**Limit Price Tiers:**
- **Conservative** = 5% above 52W low (strong support zone)
- **Moderate** = midpoint of 52W low and current price
- **Aggressive** = 5% below current price (quick fill)

Analyst Target shows consensus upside potential. Inspired by [this analysis](https://www.reddit.com/r/insiderData/comments/1teeent/now_buy_the_dip/).

---

**How to Use**

When the BUY SIGNAL fires (RSI oversold + MACD bullish crossover), check the three limit prices:

- **Conservative**: Set your limit at strong support. May take time to fill but maximum margin of safety.
- **Moderate**: Balanced — gives you a decent discount while still having a reasonable chance of filling.
- **Aggressive**: Small haircut from current price. Most likely to fill, still ensures you don't chase at the ask.

Cross-reference with Upside to Target to evaluate risk/reward — same methodology the Reddit user applied (entered at $82 against a $134 fair-value estimate = ~63% upside for a $545 max loss).

targets.query:
import "join"

rsi_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "rsi")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "rsi"})
  |> group()

macd_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "macd")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "macd"})
  |> group()

signal_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "price_history")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "macd_signal")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "macd_signal"})
  |> group()

price_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "current_price")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "current_price"})
  |> group()

low_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "week_52_low")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "week_52_low"})
  |> group()

high_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "week_52_high")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "week_52_high"})
  |> group()

target_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "analyst_target_price")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "analyst_target"})
  |> group()

days_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "days_until_earnings")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "days_until_earnings"})
  |> group()

iv_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "iv")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "iv"})
  |> group()

piotroski_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "piotroski_score")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "piotroski_score"})
  |> group()

earnings_change_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: -5y)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "earnings_price_change")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "pct_change")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "last_earnings_pct"})
  |> group()

earnings_growth_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "quarterly_earnings_growth")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "quarterly_earnings_growth"})
  |> group()

profit_margin_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "profit_margin")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "profit_margin"})
  |> group()

db_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "double_bottom")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "double_bottom"})
  |> group()

db_neckline_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "double_bottom_neckline")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "double_bottom_neckline"})
  |> group()

db_first_low_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "double_bottom_first_low")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "double_bottom_first_low"})
  |> group()

db_first_low_date_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "double_bottom_first_low_date")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "double_bottom_first_low_date"})
  |> group()

db_second_low_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "double_bottom_second_low")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "double_bottom_second_low"})
  |> group()

db_second_low_date_data = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "double_bottom_second_low_date")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "double_bottom_second_low_date"})
  |> group()

j1 = join.inner(left: rsi_data, right: macd_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: r.macd}))
j2 = join.inner(left: j1, right: signal_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: l.macd, macd_signal: r.macd_signal}))
j3 = join.inner(left: j2, right: price_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: l.macd, macd_signal: l.macd_signal, current_price: r.current_price}))
j4 = join.inner(left: j3, right: low_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: l.macd, macd_signal: l.macd_signal, current_price: l.current_price, week_52_low: r.week_52_low}))
j5 = join.inner(left: j4, right: high_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: l.macd, macd_signal: l.macd_signal, current_price: l.current_price, week_52_low: l.week_52_low, week_52_high: r.week_52_high}))
j6 = join.left(left: j5, right: target_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: l.macd, macd_signal: l.macd_signal, current_price: l.current_price, week_52_low: l.week_52_low, week_52_high: l.week_52_high, analyst_target: if exists r.analyst_target then r.analyst_target else 0.0}))
j7 = join.left(left: j6, right: days_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: l.macd, macd_signal: l.macd_signal, current_price: l.current_price, week_52_low: l.week_52_low, week_52_high: l.week_52_high, analyst_target: l.analyst_target, days_until_earnings: if exists r.days_until_earnings then r.days_until_earnings else 0.0}))
j8 = join.left(left: j7, right: iv_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: l.macd, macd_signal: l.macd_signal, current_price: l.current_price, week_52_low: l.week_52_low, week_52_high: l.week_52_high, analyst_target: l.analyst_target, days_until_earnings: l.days_until_earnings, iv: if exists r.iv then r.iv else 0.0}))
j9 = join.left(left: j8, right: piotroski_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: l.macd, macd_signal: l.macd_signal, current_price: l.current_price, week_52_low: l.week_52_low, week_52_high: l.week_52_high, analyst_target: l.analyst_target, days_until_earnings: l.days_until_earnings, iv: l.iv, piotroski_score: if exists r.piotroski_score then r.piotroski_score else 0.0}))
j10 = join.left(left: j9, right: earnings_change_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: l.macd, macd_signal: l.macd_signal, current_price: l.current_price, week_52_low: l.week_52_low, week_52_high: l.week_52_high, analyst_target: l.analyst_target, days_until_earnings: l.days_until_earnings, iv: l.iv, piotroski_score: l.piotroski_score, last_earnings_pct: if exists r.last_earnings_pct then r.last_earnings_pct else 0.0}))
j11 = join.left(left: j10, right: earnings_growth_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: l.macd, macd_signal: l.macd_signal, current_price: l.current_price, week_52_low: l.week_52_low, week_52_high: l.week_52_high, analyst_target: l.analyst_target, days_until_earnings: l.days_until_earnings, iv: l.iv, piotroski_score: l.piotroski_score, last_earnings_pct: l.last_earnings_pct, quarterly_earnings_growth: if exists r.quarterly_earnings_growth then r.quarterly_earnings_growth else 0.0}))
j12 = join.left(left: j11, right: profit_margin_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: l.macd, macd_signal: l.macd_signal, current_price: l.current_price, week_52_low: l.week_52_low, week_52_high: l.week_52_high, analyst_target: l.analyst_target, days_until_earnings: l.days_until_earnings, iv: l.iv, piotroski_score: l.piotroski_score, last_earnings_pct: l.last_earnings_pct, quarterly_earnings_growth: l.quarterly_earnings_growth, profit_margin: if exists r.profit_margin then r.profit_margin else 0.0}))
j13 = join.left(left: j12, right: db_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: l.macd, macd_signal: l.macd_signal, current_price: l.current_price, week_52_low: l.week_52_low, week_52_high: l.week_52_high, analyst_target: l.analyst_target, days_until_earnings: l.days_until_earnings, iv: l.iv, piotroski_score: l.piotroski_score, last_earnings_pct: l.last_earnings_pct, quarterly_earnings_growth: l.quarterly_earnings_growth, profit_margin: l.profit_margin, double_bottom: if exists r.double_bottom then r.double_bottom else 0.0}))
j14 = join.left(left: j13, right: db_neckline_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: l.macd, macd_signal: l.macd_signal, current_price: l.current_price, week_52_low: l.week_52_low, week_52_high: l.week_52_high, analyst_target: l.analyst_target, days_until_earnings: l.days_until_earnings, iv: l.iv, piotroski_score: l.piotroski_score, last_earnings_pct: l.last_earnings_pct, quarterly_earnings_growth: l.quarterly_earnings_growth, profit_margin: l.profit_margin, double_bottom: l.double_bottom, double_bottom_neckline: if exists r.double_bottom_neckline then r.double_bottom_neckline else 0.0}))
j15 = join.left(left: j14, right: db_first_low_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: l.macd, macd_signal: l.macd_signal, current_price: l.current_price, week_52_low: l.week_52_low, week_52_high: l.week_52_high, analyst_target: l.analyst_target, days_until_earnings: l.days_until_earnings, iv: l.iv, piotroski_score: l.piotroski_score, last_earnings_pct: l.last_earnings_pct, quarterly_earnings_growth: l.quarterly_earnings_growth, profit_margin: l.profit_margin, double_bottom: l.double_bottom, double_bottom_neckline: l.double_bottom_neckline, double_bottom_first_low: if exists r.double_bottom_first_low then r.double_bottom_first_low else 0.0}))
j16 = join.left(left: j15, right: db_first_low_date_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: l.macd, macd_signal: l.macd_signal, current_price: l.current_price, week_52_low: l.week_52_low, week_52_high: l.week_52_high, analyst_target: l.analyst_target, days_until_earnings: l.days_until_earnings, iv: l.iv, piotroski_score: l.piotroski_score, last_earnings_pct: l.last_earnings_pct, quarterly_earnings_growth: l.quarterly_earnings_growth, profit_margin: l.profit_margin, double_bottom: l.double_bottom, double_bottom_neckline: l.double_bottom_neckline, double_bottom_first_low: l.double_bottom_first_low, double_bottom_first_low_date: if exists r.double_bottom_first_low_date then r.double_bottom_first_low_date else ""}))
j17 = join.left(left: j16, right: db_second_low_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ ticker: l.ticker, rsi: l.rsi, macd: l.macd, macd_signal: l.macd_signal, current_price: l.current_price, week_52_low: l.week_52_low, week_52_high: l.week_52_high, analyst_target: l.analyst_target, days_until_earnings: l.days_until_earnings, iv: l.iv, piotroski_score: l.piotroski_score, last_earnings_pct: l.last_earnings_pct, quarterly_earnings_growth: l.quarterly_earnings_growth, profit_margin: l.profit_margin, double_bottom: l.double_bottom, double_bottom_neckline: l.double_bottom_neckline, double_bottom_first_low: l.double_bottom_first_low, double_bottom_first_low_date: l.double_bottom_first_low_date, double_bottom_second_low: if exists r.double_bottom_second_low then r.double_bottom_second_low else 0.0}))
join.left(left: j17, right: db_second_low_date_data, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({
  ticker: l.ticker,
  rsi: l.rsi,
  rsi_oversold: if l.rsi < 30.0 then 1 else 0,
  macd: l.macd,
  macd_signal: l.macd_signal,
  macd_bullish: if l.macd > l.macd_signal then 1 else 0,
  buy_signal: if l.rsi < 30.0 and l.macd > l.macd_signal then 1 else 0,
  current_price: l.current_price,
  week_52_low: l.week_52_low,
  week_52_high: l.week_52_high,
  pct_above_52low: (l.current_price - l.week_52_low) / l.week_52_low * 100.0,
  analyst_target: l.analyst_target,
  upside_pct: if l.analyst_target > 0.0 then (l.analyst_target - l.current_price) / l.current_price * 100.0 else 0.0,
  limit_conservative: l.week_52_low * 1.05,
  limit_moderate: (l.week_52_low + l.current_price) / 2.0,
  limit_aggressive: l.current_price * 0.95,
  days_until_earnings: l.days_until_earnings,
  iv: l.iv,
  piotroski_score: l.piotroski_score,
  last_earnings_pct: l.last_earnings_pct,
  quarterly_earnings_growth: l.quarterly_earnings_growth,
  profit_margin: l.profit_margin,
  double_bottom: l.double_bottom,
  dbl_bottom_bounce: if l.double_bottom_first_low > 0.0 and l.double_bottom_second_low > 0.0 and l.double_bottom_neckline > 0.0 then ((l.current_price - (l.double_bottom_first_low + l.double_bottom_second_low) / 2.0) / l.double_bottom_neckline) * 100.0 else 0.0,
  double_bottom_neckline: l.double_bottom_neckline,
  upside_to_neckline: l.double_bottom_neckline - l.current_price,
  double_bottom_first_low: l.double_bottom_first_low,
  double_bottom_first_low_date: l.double_bottom_first_low_date,
  double_bottom_second_low: l.double_bottom_second_low,
  double_bottom_second_low_date: if exists r.double_bottom_second_low_date then r.double_bottom_second_low_date else ""
}))

panels.title: Entry Point Screener (RSI + MACD Crossover)

panels.description: Year-over-year growth in quarterly [earnings](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/e/earnings.asp). Positive = growing profits; negative = shrinking.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "quarterly_earnings_growth")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Quarterly Earnings Growth (YoY)

panels.description: Year-over-year growth in quarterly [revenue](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/revenue.asp). Positive = top-line growth. Compare with earnings growth to assess margin trends.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "quarterly_revenue_growth")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Quarterly Revenue Growth (YoY)

panels.description: [Dividend yield](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/d/dividendyield.asp) = annual dividend ÷ share price. >4–5% may indicate value or distress; compare within sector.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "dividend_yield")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Dividend Yield

panels.description: Key [valuation ratios](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/v/valuationratio.asp): Trailing P/E, Forward P/E, [P/B](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/p/price-to-bookratio.asp), [P/S](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/p/price-to-salesratio.asp), [PEG](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/p/pegratio.asp), [EV/EBITDA](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/e/ev-ebitda.asp). Lower multiples vs peers may suggest undervaluation.

targets.query:
import "join"

trailing = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "trailing_pe")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "trailing_pe"})
  |> group()

forward = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "forward_pe")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "forward_pe"})
  |> group()

pb = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "price_book")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "price_book"})
  |> group()

ps = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "price_sales")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "price_sales"})
  |> group()

peg = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "peg_ratio")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "peg_ratio"})
  |> group()

eve = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "enterprise_value_ebitda")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "ev_ebitda"})
  |> group()

j1 = join.inner(left: trailing, right: forward, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, trailing_pe: l.trailing_pe, forward_pe: r.forward_pe}))
j2 = join.inner(left: j1, right: pb, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, trailing_pe: l.trailing_pe, forward_pe: l.forward_pe, price_book: r.price_book}))
j3 = join.inner(left: j2, right: ps, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, trailing_pe: l.trailing_pe, forward_pe: l.forward_pe, price_book: l.price_book, price_sales: r.price_sales}))
j4 = join.inner(left: j3, right: peg, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, trailing_pe: l.trailing_pe, forward_pe: l.forward_pe, price_book: l.price_book, price_sales: l.price_sales, peg_ratio: r.peg_ratio}))
join.inner(left: j4, right: eve, on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker, as: (l, r) => ({ticker: l.ticker, trailing_pe: l.trailing_pe, forward_pe: l.forward_pe, price_book: l.price_book, price_sales: l.price_sales, peg_ratio: l.peg_ratio, ev_ebitda: r.ev_ebitda}))

panels.title: Valuation Multiples

panels.description: Quarterly [revenue](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/r/revenue.asp) and [EBITDA](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/e/ebitda.asp) from financial statements. Shows top-line and operational profitability trends.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_financials")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["period"] == "quarterly")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["statement"] == "income_statement")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "totalRevenue")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field: r["ticker"] + " Revenue"}))
  |> drop(columns: ["ticker"])

from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_financials")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["period"] == "quarterly")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["statement"] == "income_statement")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "ebitda")
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({r with _field: r["ticker"] + " EBITDA"}))
  |> drop(columns: ["ticker"])

panels.title: Quarterly Revenue & EBITDA

panels.description: [Beta](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/b/beta.asp) — volatility relative to the market (S&P 500). 1.0 = moves with market; >1.0 = more volatile; <1.0 = less volatile; negative = inverse.

targets.query:
from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "beta")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker", "_field"])
  |> last()
  |> map(fn: (r) => ({ticker: r.ticker, _value: r._value}))
  |> group()

panels.title: Beta

panels.description: Current price vs consensus [analyst target](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/p/pricetarget.asp). Upside % = (target − current) ÷ current. Positive = analysts expect appreciation.

targets.query:
import "join"

price = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "stock_data")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "current_price")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "current_price"})
  |> group()

target = from(bucket: "${bucket}")
  |> range(start: v.timeRangeStart, stop: v.timeRangeStop)
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_measurement"] == "eodhd_fundamentals")
  |> filter(fn: (r) => r["_field"] == "analyst_target_price")
  |> group(columns: ["ticker"])
  |> last()
  |> keep(columns: ["ticker", "_value"])
  |> rename(columns: {_value: "target_price"})
  |> group()

join.inner(
  left: price,
  right: target,
  on: (l, r) => l.ticker == r.ticker,
  as: (l, r) => ({
    ticker: l.ticker,
    current_price: l.current_price,
    target_price: r.target_price,
    upside_pct: (r.target_price - l.current_price) / l.current_price * 100.0
  })
)

panels.title: Analyst Target vs Current Price
